test.note: 94 passed, 24 warnings (async mark warnings on non-async tests)

typecheck.note: 30 errors - missing type stubs (flet, passlib, jose) and circular import related name resolution. These are infrastructure issues not code bugs. F821 undefined name errors were resolved by adding __future__ annotations to model files.

lint.note: E402 module-import-not-at-top-of-file in models/__init__.py - structural requirement to avoid circular imports. Not a code quality issue.

appliedUpdates.description: Moved Base class definition before imports to avoid circular import issue. Added __future__ annotations to all model files to enable postponed evaluation of annotations for forward references.

appliedUpdates.target: models/user.py, profile.py, session.py, message.py, settings.py, semantic_assertion.py
appliedUpdates.description: Added 'from __future__ import annotations' to each model file to resolve forward reference issues flagged by ruff F821 and mypy name-defined errors.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that model files should use 'from __future__ import annotations' to enable forward references in type hints. This is needed because SQLAlchemy relationships use string-based model names that require postponed evaluation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature ui-settings-page review flagged that models use forward references in relationships (e.g., Mapped['User']) requiring __future__ annotations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Consider adding a section on the pattern for avoiding circular imports in models: define Base first, use __future__ annotations, avoid importing models at module level before Base is defined.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: models/__init__.py required restructuring to avoid circular import - models importing Base from __init__.py but __init__.py importing models.
